failure_modes.text: Precise-looking scores can hide criteria manipulation when the team really chose the answer first.
failure_modes.source_quote: criteria are subconsciously chosen or weighted to steer toward a desired conclusion
failure_modes.mitigation: Lock criteria weights before scoring alternatives; if the team adjusts weights after seeing scores, flag reverse-engineering of a predetermined answer.

failure_modes.text: Anchoring on one factor can distort the whole weighted decision even inside a formal matrix.
failure_modes.mitigation: Run a sensitivity test on the dominant criterion: check whether a plausible reweight of the top factor would flip the ranking, and if so, debate that factor first.

failure_modes.text: Analysis paralysis appears when the team keeps refining the matrix instead of making the call.
failure_modes.mitigation: Set a decision deadline before opening the matrix: agree in advance on when analysis stops and the call gets made, regardless of matrix refinement state.

premortem_questions.text: Which criterion weights would have to change materially to reverse the current ranking?
What would we have to believe for the favored option to deserve these weights?
premortem_questions.source_quote: What do we have to believe for this option to succeed?

premortem_questions.text: Is the worst case bad enough that a low-probability downside should dominate our scoring logic?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Is the worst case bad enough?

heuristics.text: Build an explicit selection matrix with weighted criteria before debating favorite options.
Start with make-or-break hypotheses and criteria rather than equal treatment of every nice-to-have factor.
Separate process quality from outcome luck so a good decision is not judged only by a single result.

curation_notes.summary: Chunk set stays close to the source's strongest failure modes, premortem questions, and repeatable heuristics.
donor_drops: Did not widen the chunk surface beyond clearly source-backed failure, premortem, and heuristic material.
open_questions: Some chunk text is compressed for operational reuse, but each item stays traceable to explicit source language.

allies: Relation-rich ally material was left for Wave 3 instead of being smuggled into chunk text.
antagonists: Bias and conflict material stronger as first-order relations was deferred to Wave 3.
structured_tensions: Any higher-order candidacy remains observational only in batch artifacts.
